mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment levels have trended upward through the current cycle
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increased month-over-month during the walk-forward test period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and discount rates sufficiently to depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices and restrictive monetary policy creates a profit-margin squeeze not captured by either variable alone; when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power relative to main effects alone (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5%, a further month-over-month decline in UNRATE signals an overheating labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 5% and declining month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates accelerating capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets efficiently price production surprises.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when INDPRO rose month-over-month during the walk-forward test period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1408 as of t-2mo, above threshold but vulnerable
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST falls below 1,200.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) contracts while consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) rise month-over-month, the resulting stagflationary environment compresses real earnings and raises input costs simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth negative and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change positive
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 contracts and CPIAUCSL rises, or the joint condition underperforms the standalone GDP signal in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy regimes where the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) triggers rapid downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with weakening consumption
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.
